title: rosa alpina subsp. laevis
intro: Rosa alpina subsp. laevis, also known as the Alpine Rose, is a subspecies of the Alpine Rose plant, a fragrant, flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Rosa alpina subsp. laevis include reducing stress and anxiety, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea, tincture, or oil, and can also be used in herbal infusions and salves to harness its medicinal properties.
